title: Data for AI
description: Learn how to prepare and validate data so AI systems receive reliable inputs.
outcomes: Explain what data quality means for AI workflows.
Load and inspect tabular data with Python tools.
Clean and transform data with reproducible steps.
Handle missing values and avoid silent data drift.
Split datasets for training and evaluation responsibly.
Create clear summaries and visuals to inform model decisions.

lessons.title: What is Data?
lessons.description: Understand how data becomes the product for AI systems.
checklist: I can explain data sources, structure, and reliability.
I can identify at least two risks in poor data.
I can name the first question to ask before modeling.
questions: How does missing data affect model behavior?
Why should data shape stay stable across versions?

lessons.title: CSV Files
lessons.description: Read and inspect CSV inputs with consistency checks.
checklist: I can read a CSV and inspect headers.
I can detect encoding and delimiter issues.
I can validate expected columns before analysis.
questions: Why do schema changes in CSV imports cause subtle bugs?
How do you protect an ETL step from malformed rows?

lessons.title: NumPy
lessons.description: Use NumPy arrays for vector-like workflows and fast operations.
checklist: I can load numeric data into arrays.
I can inspect shapes and do simple transformations.
I can spot when vectorized operations are safer.
questions: When is broadcasting a good fit in AI prep?
What does shape mismatch usually indicate?

lessons.title: Pandas
lessons.description: Manipulate tabular data and extract features from columns.
checklist: I can filter, group, and sort dataframe rows.
I can inspect missingness and types.
I can create new derived columns safely.
questions: How does copy behavior affect reproducibility in notebooks?
When should you avoid chained operations in pandas?

lessons.title: Cleaning Data
lessons.description: Build repeatable cleaning steps before modeling.
checklist: I can detect duplicate and malformed records.
I can standardize text and numeric formats.
I can document data cleaning decisions.
questions: Why is cleaning order important?
What should you log while cleaning to maintain trust?

lessons.title: Missing Values
lessons.description: Handle missing values with deliberate strategies.
checklist: I can measure missingness by feature.
I can choose fill, drop, or flag strategies.
I can explain tradeoffs of each strategy.
questions: What are the risks of deleting too many rows?
When is imputation a stronger choice than deletion?

lessons.title: Data Visualization
lessons.description: Use charts to spot outliers, distribution shifts, and patterns.
checklist: I can build at least one useful chart.
I can read spread, outliers, and class imbalance.
I can note actionable anomalies from visuals.
questions: How can a histogram reveal data quality issues?
What does outlier detection protect against in AI prep?

lessons.title: Train/Test Split
lessons.description: Create robust dataset partitions for reliable evaluation.
checklist: I can split data with seeded randomness.
I can explain why leakage breaks model trust.
I can verify class balance across splits.
questions: What happens when evaluation data leaks into training data?
Why does split ratio need contextual choices?

lessons.title: Mini Project: Data Intake Health Checker
lessons.description: Build a reusable script that validates incoming CSV health.
checklist: I can automate file loading checks.
I can produce a report of missingness and basic anomalies.
I can save outputs in a predictable format.
questions: Which checks protect the most common data breakages?
How will your checker be used by teammates?

lessons.title: Portfolio Project: Data Analysis Notebook
lessons.description: Create a short notebook that ingests, cleans, and summarizes a dataset.
checklist: I can write a notebook with reproducible cells.
I can show before/after quality improvements.
I can explain one modeling decision from data prep.
questions: How do you justify each transformation?
What baseline quality metric would you track before modeling?

lessons.title: Data for AI Quiz
lessons.description: Check readiness to move into machine learning modules.
checklist: I can describe reliable data preparation steps.
I can defend cleaning and split choices.
I can explain one quality risk and mitigation.
questions: What is the riskiest part of using external CSV data?
Why does train/test split consistency matter for model validity?

quizQuestions.prompt: In your own words, what makes data quality meaningful for AI systems?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Quality means the data is complete, consistent, and suitable for the task so models learn meaningful patterns instead of noise and artifacts.

options.label: Task-suitable consistent data
options.explanation: Quality means the data is complete, consistent, and suitable for the task so models learn meaningful patterns instead of noise and artifacts.

options.label: Raw data by default
options.explanation: Raw data often includes duplicates, missing values, bias, inconsistent formats, and noise.

options.label: Drop all missing rows
options.explanation: Dropping missing values can remove important patterns or bias the dataset.

options.label: Skip visual checks
options.explanation: A model can train on flawed data, so charts help reveal outliers, imbalance, and leakage early.

quizQuestions.prompt: What checks should you run immediately after loading a CSV for AI work?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Validate columns, inspect null counts, check types, verify delimiters and encoding, and confirm row counts are plausible.

options.label: Drop all missing rows
options.explanation: Dropping missing values can remove important patterns or bias the dataset.

options.label: Skip visual checks
options.explanation: A model can train on flawed data, so charts help reveal outliers, imbalance, and leakage early.

options.label: Columns, nulls, types, encoding
options.explanation: Validate columns, inspect null counts, check types, verify delimiters and encoding, and confirm row counts are plausible.

options.label: Reuse training data
options.explanation: Reusing the same examples for training and evaluation creates misleading performance estimates.

quizQuestions.prompt: Why are NumPy arrays useful before data cleaning at AI scale?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: They provide efficient numeric storage and vectorized math operations, helping you transform columns quickly and consistently.

options.label: Skip visual checks
options.explanation: A model can train on flawed data, so charts help reveal outliers, imbalance, and leakage early.

options.label: Efficient vectorized numeric work
options.explanation: They provide efficient numeric storage and vectorized math operations, helping you transform columns quickly and consistently.

options.label: Reuse training data
options.explanation: Reusing the same examples for training and evaluation creates misleading performance estimates.

options.label: Silent cleaning
options.explanation: Unrecorded cleaning makes results hard to debug, explain, or reproduce.

quizQuestions.prompt: How do pandas filters help keep data preparation explicit and traceable?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: You can define clear transformations in named steps, inspect intermediate outputs, and reduce ambiguity before modeling.

options.label: Reuse training data
options.explanation: Reusing the same examples for training and evaluation creates misleading performance estimates.

options.label: Silent cleaning
options.explanation: Unrecorded cleaning makes results hard to debug, explain, or reproduce.

options.label: Trust column names
options.explanation: Column names help, but values, collection context, and quality checks determine meaning.

options.label: Traceable transformation steps
options.explanation: You can define clear transformations in named steps, inspect intermediate outputs, and reduce ambiguity before modeling.

quizQuestions.prompt: Name a practical cleaning workflow and why each step exists.
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: A practical workflow is normalize schema, handle types, drop duplicates, and normalize missing values so downstream logic is stable and repeatable.

options.label: Silent cleaning
options.explanation: Unrecorded cleaning makes results hard to debug, explain, or reproduce.

options.label: Normalize, type, deduplicate
options.explanation: A practical workflow is normalize schema, handle types, drop duplicates, and normalize missing values so downstream logic is stable and repeatable.

options.label: Trust column names
options.explanation: Column names help, but values, collection context, and quality checks determine meaning.

options.label: Raw data by default
options.explanation: Raw data often includes duplicates, missing values, bias, inconsistent formats, and noise.

quizQuestions.prompt: When should you avoid dropping all rows with missing values?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Avoid dropping all missing rows when missingness is high or carries signal, because you may lose representativeness and introduce hidden bias.

options.label: Trust column names
options.explanation: Column names help, but values, collection context, and quality checks determine meaning.

options.label: Raw data by default
options.explanation: Raw data often includes duplicates, missing values, bias, inconsistent formats, and noise.

options.label: Drop all missing rows
options.explanation: Dropping missing values can remove important patterns or bias the dataset.

options.label: Preserve signal and reduce bias
options.explanation: Avoid dropping all missing rows when missingness is high or carries signal, because you may lose representativeness and introduce hidden bias.

quizQuestions.prompt: How do charts help diagnose data quality issues before modeling?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Charts reveal skew, outliers, imbalance, and anomalies quickly, allowing you to detect issues before feature engineering or training.

options.label: Raw data by default
options.explanation: Raw data often includes duplicates, missing values, bias, inconsistent formats, and noise.

options.label: Drop all missing rows
options.explanation: Dropping missing values can remove important patterns or bias the dataset.

options.label: Skip visual checks
options.explanation: A model can train on flawed data, so charts help reveal outliers, imbalance, and leakage early.

options.label: Charts reveal data problems
options.explanation: Charts reveal skew, outliers, imbalance, and anomalies quickly, allowing you to detect issues before feature engineering or training.

quizQuestions.prompt: What does a clean train/test split protect against in evaluation?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: A clean split prevents leakage, gives realistic performance estimates, and keeps model behavior evaluation honest.

options.label: Drop all missing rows
options.explanation: Dropping missing values can remove important patterns or bias the dataset.

options.label: Skip visual checks

options.label: Reuse training data

options.label: Prevent leakage in evaluation
options.explanation: A clean split prevents leakage, gives realistic performance estimates, and keeps model behavior evaluation honest.
